issues.description: FR-3a claims 'at least one profile' invariant is implied by 'can't remove active profile + must always have an active profile', but this invariant does NOT hold. `profile add` explicitly does NOT set `current_profile`, so a user can: (1) add a profile (current_profile stays None), (2) remove that profile (it's not active, so the guard doesn't fire), (3) end up with zero profiles. The invariant claim is incorrect as stated.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) make `profile add` automatically set `current_profile` when it's the first profile added (like `docker context create` does), or (b) add an explicit guard in `profile rm` that rejects removal when it would leave zero profiles, or (c) remove the invariant claim and accept that zero-profile state is valid (it already has error handling via FR-1c cold-start).

issues.description: FR-6a says `config --set server_url=X` writes to the ACTIVE profile, but doesn't specify behavior when `current_profile` is None (cold-start state with no profiles). The config command is early-dispatched and may encounter this state. Should it error? Auto-create a profile? The spec is silent.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit behavior: `nemo config --set <profile-key>=<value>` with no active profile errors with the same cold-start message from FR-1c: 'No profiles configured. Run nemo profile add ...' This is consistent with the rest of the spec.

issues.description: FR-3a mentions a `--switch` flag on `profile add` ('Does NOT switch to it; use use-profile separately. --switch flag combines both.') but FR-3b, which is the detailed flag specification for `profile add`, does not list `--switch` among the flags. An implementer following FR-3b strictly would omit it.
issues.suggestion: Add `--switch` to the FR-3b flag list with description: '`--switch` (optional, default false): after creating the profile, set it as the active profile (equivalent to running `nemo use-profile <name>` after add).'

issues.description: FR-3a `profile rename <old> <new>` doesn't specify behavior for `nemo profile rename foo foo` (renaming to same name). Technically `<new>` already exists (it IS the profile being renamed), so the 'errors if new already exists' guard would fire, producing a confusing error.
issues.suggestion: Add: 'If `<old>` and `<new>` are identical, the command is a no-op (no error, no write).' Or clarify that the existence check excludes the source profile.

issues.description: No way to retrieve `current_profile` name via `config --get`. The FR-6c allow-list only includes profile-scoped and root-scoped keys. Scripts wanting to determine the active profile must parse `nemo profile ls` output.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding `current_profile` as a gettable (read-only) key in FR-6g, or document that `nemo profile ls` (with machine-parseable output or a `--json` flag) is the intended way to query this.

issues.description: FR-2a migration message and FR-3a/FR-5a informational output don't specify whether messages go to stdout or stderr. For CLI tools, informational/diagnostic messages should go to stderr so stdout remains clean for piping (e.g., `nemo config --get server_url` piped to another command shouldn't include migration noise).
issues.suggestion: Add a note that migration messages and status banners (FR-5a profile header) go to stderr, while data output (--get values, profile ls listing) goes to stdout. Alternatively, state that all non-data output goes to stderr.

issues.description: FR-3a `profile ls` and `use-profile` describe content but not exact output format. For `ls`, the spec says 'one per line, active marked *, include server_url and engineer' but doesn't show a sample. This leaves room for inconsistent implementations but is probably fine for v1.
issues.suggestion:
Add a sample output block for `profile ls`, e.g.:
```
  default   http://localhost:18080        dev
* work      https://nautiloop.work.internal ggylfason
  personal  http://100.64.1.10:8080        gunnar
```
